Changing Enviorments, One Line at a Time.

Aren Vista

field: Software Engineer

Insert some witty quote here.

About

A little about my work

View full resume ↗

Hi! I'm a guy who likes making stuff — too many things, really. I find, design, and build things to make my life a little easier and to explore my many hobbies. This site is where I share what I know, in the hopes I get to see some cool things back. I live out on the East Coast with my wonderful girlfriend and one crazy dog, and work hard to keep myself supplied in coffee. Much of what's here is really just for me — and for my students, who keep asking for my material.

Outside of work I write stories, paint, and bake pottery with friends.

Languages
  • Python 3
  • C++17

Experience

Where I've worked

  1. Undergraduate Researcher — Mathematics
    Bedrich Sousedik · UMBC • Dec 2025 – Present
    • Research optimized betting strategies using Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) methods.
    • Develop probabilistic models to evaluate risk, expected return, and long-term strategy performance.
  2. Undergraduate Researcher — Computer Engineering
    Riadul Islam · UMBC • Dec 2025 – Present
    • Develop spiking neural networks (SNNs) for classification of temporal data.
    • Design convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for image classification tasks.
  3. Project Lead — Computational Biologist
    National Institutes of Health • Jun 2021 – Jun 2023
    • Established a Git-based version control system to coordinate development across labs.
    • Applied K-Means and DBSCAN to cluster multivariate data for anomaly detection and disease pathology.
    • Built automated HPC/SWARM workflows and a CLI tool to accelerate 3D amino acid structure generation.
  4. Undergraduate Researcher — Organic Chemistry
    Seley Radtke Lab · UMBC • Jun 2017 – Jun 2020
    • Curated a database of approved nucleoside analogues using Python, BeautifulSoup, and Selenium.
    • Designed a synthetic pathway for N-glycosylated imidazole 4-boronic acids, increasing yields by 40%.
Recognition
Publications & Acknowledgements
  • Publication: Vasta, G. R., et al. (2020). Biochemical Characterization of Oyster and Clam Galectins. Frontiers in Chemistry, 8, 98.
  • Acknowledgement: Brown, S. M., et al. (2025). Mapping the Edges of Mass Spectral Prediction. Analytical Chemistry, 97(19).

Selected work

Field specimens

Keep scrolling — every frame is running live. Click one to open it.

Game of Life

Conway's Game of Life implemented in C++ using Raylib — cellular automata at 60fps.

fig. 01 — 2025
  • C++
  • Raylib

Audio Recommendation

A music recommender using aligned audio and lyric embeddings to bypass metadata and collaborative filtering.

fig. 02 — 2025
  • Python
  • PyTorch

SnapDaemon

Software implementation of Wooting keyboards' SnapTap feature — last-input-wins key resolution as a background daemon.

fig. 03 — 2024
  • C++
  • Linux

TinyOS

A tiny operating system written in x86 assembly and C++ — bootloader to shell. Work in progress.

fig. 04 — 2023
  • ASM
  • C++

ChessC++

Command-line chess engine in C++ using Ncurses — my first real exposure to programming.

fig. 05 — 2025
  • C++
  • Ncurses

Math-o-nomicon

A LaTeX compendium covering topics from Numerical Linear Algebra to Real Analysis.

fig. 06 — 2024
  • LaTeX

Archive

Everything, indexed

The full collection, filterable by stack.

  1. 01 Game of Life ✳ Conway's Game of Life implemented in C++ using Raylib — cellular automata at 60fps. 2025 ↗ Source
  2. 02 Audio Recommendation ✳ A music recommender using aligned audio and lyric embeddings to bypass metadata and collaborative filtering. 2025 ↗ Source
  3. 03 SnapDaemon Software implementation of Wooting keyboards' SnapTap feature — last-input-wins key resolution as a background daemon. 2024 ↗ Source
  4. 04 TinyOS A tiny operating system written in x86 assembly and C++ — bootloader to shell. Work in progress. 2023 ↗ Source
  5. 05 ChessC++ Command-line chess engine in C++ using Ncurses — my first real exposure to programming. 2025 ↗ Source
  6. 06 Math-o-nomicon A LaTeX compendium covering topics from Numerical Linear Algebra to Real Analysis. 2024 ↗ Source
  7. 07 Blood on the Clocktower Discord bot that helps run the board game 'Blood on the Clocktower' — roles, nominations, night order. 2023 ↗ Source
  8. 08 TextEdit Terminal-based text editor in C++ using Ncurses. 2024 ↗ Source
  9. 09 Mango Nana Storefront selling figurines and sculptures — Astro, TSX, and Stripe. 2023 ↗ Source
  10. 10 portfolio.me ✳ This site — Astro + TypeScript with live canvas work previews, hosted on AWS. 2026 ↗ Source
  11. 11 ASCAutopayroll A Selenium tool automating UMBC student timesheets — auto-filling PeopleSoft. 2022 ↗ Source
Writing

Random Thoughts.

All posts →
  • Mar 19, 2026

    The nested radical that equals exactly 2

    Evaluating √(2 + √(2 + √(2 + ⋯))) the honest way — prove it converges first, then take the limit — plus a surprise cameo from cosine.

     Read post →
  • Mar 12, 2026

    Planetary Parade: turning a probability nightmare into Euler's formula

    Jane Street's March 2026 puzzle about spotting six alien planets at once. Conditional probabilities are hopeless — but counting regions on a sphere is not.

     Read post →
  • Feb 26, 2026

    The pigeonhole proof hiding in every list of numbers

    Any n natural numbers contain a consecutive block whose sum is divisible by n — and the proof is prefix sums plus pigeonhole.

     Read post →
